title: Crossopetalum theoides
intro: Crossopetalum theoides, also known as the "Mayan tea tree," is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Crossopetalum theoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used to make a tincture, which is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

The genus Crossopetalum is a part of this family and consists of tropical and subtropical species. Crossopetalum theoides is native to Central and South America, where it grows as a shrub or a small tree.
